acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `cargo check` passes on workspace
- [ ] All 5 new crates listed in workspace.members
- [ ] workspace.package.version = "0.2.0-alpha"
- [ ] All crates use version.workspace = true
- [ ] env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION") returns same value in all crates
description:
**Problem**
Need workspace structure before extracting any crates.

**Changes**
1. Update `/Cargo.toml` to add workspace members:
   - crates/beads-macros
   - crates/beads-core
   - crates/beads-api
   - crates/beads-surface

2. Add workspace versioning at `0.2.0-alpha`:
   ```toml
   [workspace.package]
   version = "0.2.0-alpha"
   ```

3. Create stub crates with minimal Cargo.toml and empty lib.rs:
   - `crates/beads-macros/Cargo.toml` + `src/lib.rs`
   - `crates/beads-core/Cargo.toml` + `src/lib.rs`
   - `crates/beads-api/Cargo.toml` + `src/lib.rs`
   - `crates/beads-surface/Cargo.toml` + `src/lib.rs`

4. Set `version.workspace = true` in ALL crates (including beads-rs)

**Files**
- /Cargo.toml (workspace)
- crates/beads-macros/Cargo.toml + src/lib.rs (new)
- crates/beads-core/Cargo.toml + src/lib.rs (new)
- crates/beads-api/Cargo.toml + src/lib.rs (new)
- crates/beads-surface/Cargo.toml + src/lib.rs (new)
- crates/beads-rs/Cargo.toml (update version)
title: Phase 0: Workspace scaffolding and versioning

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] It is impossible to represent seq>0 with unknown head in the durable/applied watermarks used for contiguity.
- [ ] Gap buffer/drain paths no longer need PrevUnknown runtime checks for durable head.
- [ ] Tests cover head-known enforcement and any compatibility behavior.
description:
**Problem**
Watermark allows HeadStatus::Unknown for seq>0 (core/watermark.rs). Plan §0.12 says head sha must be known for seq>0. Today this invariant is enforced ad hoc (gap_buffer drain returns PrevUnknown), which is brittle and forces runtime checks across the replication path.

**Files:** src/core/watermark.rs, src/daemon/store_runtime.rs, src/daemon/repl/session.rs, src/daemon/repl/gap_buffer.rs, src/daemon/repl/runtime.rs
design:
**Design**
- Introduce a head-known watermark type (e.g., Watermark<K, HeadKnown>) or split Watermark into Known/Maybe variants.
- Restrict contiguity-critical APIs (advance_contiguous, gap buffer drain) to require head-known watermarks.
- Decide how to handle peers that omit head hashes: either reject at protocol boundary or store them in a separate "unknown head" map until upgraded.
- Update load_watermarks to reject/flag seq>0 without head unless in a compatibility mode.
title: Make head-knownness explicit in watermarks

acceptance_criteria:
**Acceptance**
- [ ] All handshake/streaming operations are type‑gated (cannot compile in the wrong phase/role).
- [ ] `session.peer()`/`negotiated_max_frame_bytes()` are only available after handshake.
- [ ] The repl loops no longer track `streaming` or `accepted_set` separately; they derive from the session type/state.
- [ ] It is impossible to send events or hot‑cache frames unless the session is in Streaming.
- [ ] Tests cover legal transitions and ensure illegal transitions are unrepresentable (compile‑fail or typestate tests).
description:
**Problem**
The replication session state machine is enforced by runtime checks and shadow state outside the session. The compiler cannot prevent illegal transitions or misuse.

Specific compiler‑blind spots:
- Session phase/role gating is runtime only (`begin_handshake`, `resend_handshake`, `handle_message`). A wrong call sequence compiles and just returns None/error.
- Both inbound and outbound loops track `streaming` as a separate bool (and `accepted_set` out‑of‑band). Once set, it never goes false even if the session transitions to Draining/Closed. This allows sending events/hot‑cache in invalid phases without compile‑time or runtime guardrails.
- Code that depends on negotiated peer info uses `session.peer()` at runtime and falls back to config when missing; this is a smell that the type system doesn’t model “handshake completed” as a state.

Key refs:
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/repl/session.rs:226` (handshake API), `:251` (message handling)
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/repl/manager.rs:414` / `:528` (shadow `streaming` + `accepted_set`)
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/repl/server.rs:484` / `:596` (same issue inbound)

**Impact**
A single refactor or new feature can accidentally send events before handshake or after draining/close. These bugs will compile and only appear at runtime under load. This is correctness‑critical for repl.
design:
**Design (opinionated)**
Make the session phase/role a *type boundary* and collapse shadow state into the session type.

1) Introduce typestated session:
```rust
struct Session<R, P> { /* fields */ }
// R: Inbound | Outbound, P: Connecting | Handshaking | Streaming | Draining | Closed
```
- Each phase exposes only valid operations.
- `begin_handshake(self) -> (Session<Outbound, Handshaking>, SessionAction)`
- `handle_hello(self, ...) -> Session<Inbound, Streaming>` etc.

2) Encode “peer known” in the type:
- Only `Session<*, Streaming>` (or `StreamingSession` wrapper) exposes `peer()` and `negotiated_max_frame_bytes()`.
- Remove fallbacks on config when peer is None; make it impossible to call those methods pre‑handshake.

3) Remove shadow `streaming` + `accepted_set`:
- The loop should match on the session typestate.
- `accepted_set` is derived from `StreamingSession`’s peer data, not a separately mutated local variable.
- Event send/hot‑cache methods accept `&StreamingSession` only.

4) Phase transitions are explicit:
- `SessionAction` can include a typed next‑state or return `(Session<NewState>, Vec<Action>)` to make transitions explicit and compiler‑checked.

This turns invalid phase usage into compile‑time errors.
title: Repl session typestate + streaming gating

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `setup` has a clearer path to supporting more than the current three environments.
- [ ] At least one tranche of additional editor/agent recipes is implemented or the extensibility scaffold is in place with tests.
- [ ] Help output makes supported environments and check/remove flows obvious.
description:
**Problem**
beads-rs `setup` currently covers only a small editor set, while beads-go has a broader recipe model and install/check/remove ergonomics for multiple AI-editor environments. The current rs surface works, but it does not yet scale as the project adds more editor targets.

**Files:** crates/beads-cli/src/commands/setup.rs, any new template/recipe support added under crates/beads-cli/src/**
design: Evolve `setup` from a handful of hardcoded subcommands into a recipe-oriented or otherwise extensible system, then add the first tranche of missing environments. Keep install/check/remove semantics consistent and avoid rewriting the command every time a new editor is added.
title: Expand  beyond the current editor trio
